verdict: 2026-07-01 13:16:29 PDT — The overall shape is sound as one story, not a split: the workflow files, branch protection, allow_auto_merge, and docs are coupled by /close's MODE=auto/direct/abort behavior, and gh-api read-back is a reasonable lightweight way to verify non-file repo settings in this repo. The sketch needs a tighter branch-protection rollout and security posture before it is a real backstop. External source checks used: GitHub branch protection REST docs, protected-branch docs, and GitHub Actions secure-use docs.

findings.title: Admin bypass leaves the promised backstop cooperative
findings.claim: The sketch keeps enforce_admins off because this is a solo repo and /close needs to merge, but README and ARCHITECTURE explicitly say the local hook is only a cooperative tripwire and server-side branch protection is the real backstop. With admin enforcement off, the admin token driving gh can still bypass required PR/checks, including the documented hook gap for destination-refspec pushes from a feature branch. /close's PR auto-merge path does not need admin bypass when checks pass.
findings.alternative: Set enforce_admins=true, avoid bypass allowances, and express the PR requirement with zero required GitHub reviews if Thomas's approval remains outside GitHub. If an emergency bypass is desired, document it as a manual owner action outside the light workflow.
findings.win: Makes the stated invariant true for the actual operator, closes the known local-hook gaps server-side, and preserves the normal /close MODE=auto path.

findings.title: Required check is assumed before it is observed
findings.claim: The self-validating plan assumes a single job named gate is the required status-check context, then uses branch-protection read-back as proof. That is brittle: GitHub required checks depend on the exact emitted check name, duplicate job names can make checks ambiguous, and the REST API now provides app-bound checks in addition to legacy contexts. A wrong required context can read back as configured while leaving auto-merge stuck or blocking the first CI PR.
findings.alternative: Keep one story, but make it a two-phase rollout: add and push the workflows, observe the actual PR check name/check-run from GitHub, then configure branch protection with a literal payload using checks/app_id where available, an explicit strict choice, and required_pull_request_reviews.required_approving_review_count=0. Verify both the protection read-back and that the current PR has that exact required check green.
findings.win: Removes the chicken-and-egg failure mode, prevents context-name mismatch, and proves /close is waiting on the same check branch protection actually requires.

findings.title: Action pinning is under-specified for a security boundary
findings.claim: The sketch says actions are version-pinned, which still permits tag pins such as @v4 or @v2. For a new CI/security backstop, tag pins leave a supply-chain path open if an action tag is moved or the action publisher is compromised, especially for the third-party gitleaks action.
findings.alternative: Require full commit-SHA pins for all external actions, with a comment naming the upstream release, and set minimal workflow permissions such as contents: read. If using a binary instead of an action, verify the release checksum.
findings.win: Hardens the new trust boundary without adding local dependencies, extra scripts, or complexity to the repo's minimal gate.
